response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and web developer with over 15 years of experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure is narrow and dated — a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor thesis project — while his career trajectory has moved decisively toward TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies. The role demands deep, current C++ expertise covering STL, multi-threading, message brokers, and IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices in an industrial automation context, none of which appear in his recent work. The industrial automation, MQTT, OPC UA, and cybersecurity dimensions are entirely absent from his background.
strengths: Broad distributed-systems and event-driven architecture experience (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second)
Advantageous-skill overlap: React, Node.js, and TypeScript are listed as beneficial and are core to his recent work
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing culture through workshops, code kata sessions, and community talks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; no evidence of STL mastery, concurrent C++ programming, or high-performance systems work in C++
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or any industrial cybersecurity standards
No people-management, architectural leadership, or senior-manager-level responsibilities evident despite the mid-senior managerial seniority of the role
